assertion_results.text: The plan determines feature-branch workflow mode (not direct-to-main) based on the atomicity constraints in the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md states 'Mode: feature-branch' with rationale: 'the migration must be atomic (partial migration leaves the database inconsistent), and all code changes are coupled with the migration... All changes must land together via a feature branch.' This directly references the atomicity constraints from the feature description's Non-Functional Requirements.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type create-branch is generated as the first task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-1-create-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\ncreate-branch' and is numbered as Task 1, the first task in the plan.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type merge-branch is generated as the last task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-7-merge-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\nmerge-branch' and is numbered as Task 7, the last of 7 total tasks.

assertion_results.text: All intermediate tasks (between the create-branch and merge-branch bookend tasks) have Target Branch set to TC-9005
assertion_results.evidence: All five intermediate tasks have Target Branch set to TC-9005: task-2 (line 8), task-3 (line 8), task-4 (line 8), task-5 (line 8), task-6 (line 8).

assertion_results.text: The create-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
The merge-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
All intermediate tasks list the create-branch bookend task as a dependency
assertion_results.evidence: All five intermediate tasks list Task 1 as a dependency: task-2 line 44 'Depends on: Task 1', task-3 line 39 'Depends on: Task 1', task-4 line 56 'Depends on: Task 1', task-5 line 35 'Depends on: Task 1', task-6 line 41 'Depends on: Task 1'.

assertion_results.text: The merge-branch bookend task lists all intermediate tasks as dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: task-7-merge-feature-branch.md lines 22-26 list all five intermediate tasks: 'Depends on: Task 2', 'Depends on: Task 3', 'Depends on: Task 4', 'Depends on: Task 5', 'Depends on: Task 6'.

assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 (task-1-create-feature-branch.md) is missing 'Files to Modify', 'Files to Create', and 'Implementation Notes' sections. Task 7 (task-7-merge-feature-branch.md) is missing 'Files to Modify', 'Files to Create', and 'Implementation Notes' sections. These bookend tasks contain only Repository, Target Branch, Bookend Type, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Dependencies.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes a workflow:feature-branch label decision to be applied to the feature issue
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 7: 'The feature requires the `workflow:feature-branch` label on the feature issue.'

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: digests.md contains exactly 7 lines (one per task), each in the format '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex-chars>'. All hashes are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters. Example: 'sha256-md:e6d0d3f10f1c691e4b4c3686c5d34144bfe9e24d7fdfe9603759f2ccbf2d3585'.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention references in Implementation Notes follow the prescribed format. Task 3: 'Applies: task modifies `entity/src/advisory.rs` matching the convention\'s SeaORM scope.' Task 4: four conventions, all with 'Applies: task modifies <specific file> matching the convention\'s <scope>.' Task 5: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs` matching the convention\'s `.rs` handler scope.' Task 6: 'Applies: task modifies `tests/api/advisory.rs` matching the convention\'s test file scope.' No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales found.
